Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, C13 — p.54.

A single low desk sits against the wall of this room. A large wooden door marked with a painting of a spider stands in the southern wall.

Devargo's sea chest (his key, or @Check[type:thievery|dc:30]) holds his favourite treasures, re-scaled to PF2e: a jasper-studded amulet and an emerald gold necklace (art objects, ~120 gp the pair), a ring of feather fall with a jade-dragonfly cameo, a scroll of blur, an elixir of love, two doses of dust of appearance, a mother-of-pearl horn (~10 gp), and ~60 gp in coin.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, C14 — p.54.

Devargo retires here after midnight to read the day's slate reports and sleep — the best time to infiltrate Eel's End. The ambassador's blackmail letters are here (in his footlocker), the entire object of the mission. A stealth route avoids the throne-room confrontation entirely.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, C15 — p.54-55.

This dark chamber is riddled with dizzying tunnels and twisting corridors formed by thick cobwebs. The floor is a sticky, lumpy mass of webbing and dozens, if not hundreds, of bones-many of which appear to be from humanoid bodies. Some of the webs seem to shimmer and dance, almost as if reflecting rainbows in their silken forms.

The lair of @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.chittersnap00001]{Chittersnap}, the ettercap who is the true source of Devargo's 'spider control,' beneath the throne's trap door. The shimmering strands are dream-spider webs: every square is difficult terrain and a @Check[type:reflex|dc:10] each round avoids their contact poison.

D. The Dead Warrens — a derro-infested undercity laboratory beneath Potter's Field, the last chapter site (the PCs recover the murdered Shoanti boy Gaekhen's body here for Event 13). Tracks at the surface (@Check[type:perception|dc:15], @Check[type:nature|dc:20] to read them as derro) lead to a trap-door in a mausoleum.

GM: This is one of several labs kept by the necromancer Rolth (Gaedren Lamm's estranged son, the 'Key-Lock Killer'). Rolth is absent — away securing a scroll — and is not scheduled to appear; he returns after the PCs leave to find his lair wrecked, and hunts them in Chapter 2. In his stead the warrens are run by his derro apprentice @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vreegDerroNec001]{Vreeg}.

Gaekhen's body is in four parts (Rolth harvested only the head): the legs in the corpse dump (@UUID[.lN66rXLQlv1qqJtX]{D4}), the torso + right arm animated in the stitchery (@UUID[.7I11UQ9DftM2qYWA]{D11}), the left arm clutched by the carrion golem (@UUID[.TnexBuoE8QRzKO7Q]{D8}), and the head stitched to the flesh-golem in @UUID[.7I11UQ9DftM2qYWA]{D11}. Thousand Bones needs all four parts, not a whole body.

Running the difficulty. In PF2e a lone at-level creature reads as Trivial–Low for four PCs, so most warren rooms are individually easy — the dungeon is an attrition gauntlet, not a string of set-piece fights. Lean into the design's own reinforcement: a @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.derroMinion00001]{derro} who spots the party flees the @UUID[.YynfCuegJ7nCn8Ex]{crawl-spaces} to pull its kin (combine @UUID[.fpStLjsEeFJmTps8]{D3}/@UUID[.H53AzWJ2Y7cD62oP]{D5}/@UUID[.PB1epZ2aNEyqBMJs]{D7} derros into one Low–Moderate fight), and the @UUID[.nAWa4i9BCByO7asi]{ossuary} skeletons pursue — add them to a later room. The climax — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vreegDerroNec001]{Vreeg} in @UUID[.q9TYHeGnxKqUNIRn]{D13} — is a true Severe boss.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, D1 — p.56.

This large room is supported by four wide pillars of stone. The ceiling above arches in a dome nearly twenty feet high. The walls are lined with skeletons caked into the mudadult human bones mostly, but here and there smaller bones might be from halflings or perhaps children. Fifteenfoot-square pits sit to the east and west, each filled with a large heap of hundreds of bones. To the south, a crude hole has been gouged out of the wall, providing access to a tunnel.

An abandoned ossuary. Rolth animated guardians in the bone pits — four @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.trchDxbDR2TiPMxT]{human skeletons} (west) and a @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.skeletalOwlbr001]{skeletal owlbear} (east), spotted before they rise with @Check[type:perception|dc:15]. The skeletons pursue throughout the warrens but never up into Potter's Ward.

Six malnourished captives await Rolth's experiments. Their jailer is the ogrekin @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cabbageheadOgr01]{Cabbagehead}, who torments them to please his 'father' Rolth and fights to the death.

Two wide, freestanding bookshelves furnish this room. The shelves are filled with row upon row of carefully arranged tomes and scrolls.

Mostly necromancy and golem-craft treatises — plus a surprising number of books on disease and plague (a +2 circumstance bonus to relevant Recall Knowledge while using them; ominous foreshadowing of Chapter 2). The collection is worth ~30 gp re-scaled; tucked in an anatomy folio are a scroll of identify and a scroll of command undead.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, D11 — p.59.

The nauseating mixture of decay and strange chemicals fills the air of this large room. Glinting saws, pliers, long stitching needles, and other surgical equipment are organized on shelves and benches along the walls. A sturdy wooden table in the center of the room supports a large humanoid shape-a thing stitched together from a patchwork of dozens of different bodies. The thing would stand nearly seven feet tall if it rose.

Rolth's workshop. The strapped-down flesh golem is unfinished; the small head stitched to it — brown hair, a scar on the left cheek — is Gaekhen's head (the fourth and last body part). The torso + right arm Vreeg claimed and crudely animated (2 HP, +0 unarmed for 1d3 nonlethal, 50% miss from blindness; harmed by vitality) — it thrashes if handled.

On the desk: a broken key-shaped dagger — Rolth's signature blade, twin to the one in @UUID[.9PUuNDyWIkXSwDeP]{Gaedren's den (A14)}, confirming the connection. The PCs may not grasp its meaning until they meet Rolth in Chapter 2.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, D12 — p.59.

This chamber holds a large four-poster bed and a simple writing desk, along with a full-length mirror propped up against one earthen wall.

Rolth keeps little here. @Check[type:perception|dc:20] (automatic if a PC looks behind the mirror) reveals a hidden niche: masterwork thieves' tools, a pearl-and-silver snuffbox (~25 gp re-scaled) holding a dose of dust of disappearance, and a sustaining spoon. His spellbooks are not here — he carries them.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, D13 — p.59-61.

This chamber is part cluttered study, part nest — borrowed library books, half-finished necromantic tinkering, and the lingering chill of worked death magic.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vreegDerroNec001]{Vreeg} the derro necromancer lairs here, attended by a single @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{human zombie} bodyguard left by Rolth. He kites with rays and force missiles and saves Blindness for the front line.

The gut-punch. If the PCs left @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm's} body in the fishery, this zombie is Gaedren — recovered and animated by his son Rolth. Mindless, it won't know them, but its appearance should unsettle the party and raise the question of who wanted the old man back in undeath.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vreegDerroNec001]{Vreeg} (the warrens' master) and his @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Xo4IGzw28hivgMmM]{zombie} guard. Bright light leaves Vreeg dazzled (derro sunlight vulnerability) — a real lever for the PCs.

With Vreeg down and the four body parts gathered, the warrens are cleared. Rolth returns later to the ruin and becomes a Chapter 2 antagonist (CHG: his vendetta).

Chapter Conclusion. With the Dead Warrens cleared and the scapegoat's fate resolved, 'Edge of Anarchy' ends. The PCs should be well into 4th level.

Returning Gaekhen. Deliver Gaekhen's four body parts to @UUID[.lAEtkmbh1g2svBUJ]{Thousand Bones} (Event 13). The grateful Shoanti elder's goodwill becomes important in Chapter 4 (A History of Ashes). Cressida pays the recovery reward (folding in Trinia's deferred coin if applicable).

The execution. Queen Ileosa's public execution of her scapegoat (@UUID[.FZ53VK84dpCVeiiv]{Event 14}) is the chapter's final beat — disrupt it, attend it, or let it stand. Either way the city quiets, and the PCs are now known to the queen.

Into Chapter 2 — and the Blood Veil. As order returns, the first cases of a new plague appear in the gutters. This is where the persistent-plague overlay begins: start the Epidemic Clock (Conversion Guide journal → Blood Veil & the Epidemic Clock) seeded by how the city fared here. Rolth, returning to his ruined lair, becomes a recurring hunter (Chapter 2).

Threads carried forward: Rolth's vendetta · the Arkona/Vimanda watch (from @UUID[.klh1VxaRSp2p6D65]{Verik}) · Trinia & Vencarlo/Blackjack · Grau and the Korvosan Guard · the Shoanti debt · and the queen, who now has the PCs' measure.
